summary: iOS 👀 FALCON | DFU - App incorrectly shows device upto date after failure
scenario: App incorrectly shows headset as up to date after DFU failure, preventing retry and falsely marking both headset and charge stand as updated
description_clean: After a DFU (Device Firmware Update) failure for the headset, the app incorrectly displays that the device is up to date. When the user attempts to retry the update, the app shows "update unavailable." Later, the main SW update section shows both the headset and charge stand as successfully updated to the latest build, but the headset was actually not updated.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the Android device.
2. Navigate to the Home screen and ensure the headset is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Software Update.
4. Initiate a Device Firmware Update (DFU) for the headset.
5. Allow the DFU process to fail (e.g., by interrupting the connection or powering off the headset during the update).
6. Observe the status displayed on the Software Update screen after the failure.
7. Attempt to retry the update by tapping the update button again.
8. Observe the message shown when attempting to retry.
9. Navigate back to the main Software Update section and observe the status for both the headset and the charge stand.
expected: After a DFU failure, the app should display a failure status (e.g., "Update Failed") and allow the user to retry the update. The main Software Update section should accurately reflect that the headset was not updated to the latest build.
actual: After a DFU failure, the app incorrectly displays that the device is up to date. When retrying, the app shows "update unavailable." The main SW update section shows both the headset and charge stand as successfully updated to the latest build, even though the headset was not actually updated.
